source: Label 309 verifier pipeline — transaction-reference integrity binding
description: Before reading anything out of fetched transaction bytes, a verifier MUST recompute blake2b-256 over the fetched transaction-body bytes (the transaction id, by ledger definition) against the requested hash, and blake2b-256 over the fetched auxiliary-data bytes against the verified body's auxiliary_data_hash field — both over the bytes exactly as fetched, never a re-encoding. The transaction bodies here are synthetic minimal CBOR (not ledger-valid transactions); the hashing inputs and outputs are what is pinned.

vectors.name: binding-ok
vectors.note: blake2b-256 over the transaction-body bytes exactly as fetched equals the requested transaction hash, and blake2b-256 over the auxiliary-data bytes equals the body's auxiliary_data_hash (key 7); the record body then reassembles from the label-309 chunk array.

vectors.name: requested-tx-hash-mismatch
vectors.note: The fetched body bytes hash to a different transaction id than requested; the response carries provably wrong bytes and MUST be discarded before anything is read out of it.

vectors.name: auxiliary-data-tampered
vectors.note: One flipped byte inside the auxiliary data fails the auxiliary_data_hash binding even though the transaction-body hash still matches.

vectors.name: auxiliary-data-hash-field-absent
vectors.note: A response whose verified body carries no auxiliary_data_hash while auxiliary data is present fails the same binding: such a transaction cannot exist on chain.

vectors.name: bound-transaction-without-label-309
vectors.note: Both bindings hold, but the integrity-bound transaction carries no metadata under label 309 — a record-attributable outcome proven by the transaction itself.
